text: To scan a network stealthily without completing the three-way handshake, you run a {{c1::TCP SYN scan}} (often called a half-open scan).
explanation: A SYN scan sends a SYN packet and waits for a SYN-ACK. If received, it immediately sends a RST (reset) packet to close the connection without completing the handshake (no ACK is sent). This makes the scan faster and less likely to trigger application-level connection logs.
spanish: Para escanear una red sigilosamente sin completar el saludo de tres vías, ejecutas un escaneo TCP SYN (a menudo llamado escaneo medio abierto).
